programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Sour wishes she could have a birthday all her very own, her twin sister Sweet grants her wish by forgoing celebrating her birthday. But what begins as a day that Sour has always dreamed of quickly becomes a disappointment when she misses her twin. With Strawberry's help, Sour gives her birthday back to Sweet with a special surprise.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When a comedy of errors causes Orange, Plum and Cherry to arrive late to their campout, they start off their annual tradition of telling tall-tales around the campfire.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: At the girls' campout, Sour launches into a whopper of a yarn about the adventures she and Sweet had in the "Berry Deep Dark Forest." She tries to get Sweet to participate in spinning the tale, but Sour's over-the-top performance leaves Sweet behind. Toward the end, Sour graciously changes the story to pull Sweet into it, giving Sweet the opportunity to toss in an ad lib that makes a nice topper to Sour's story.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Apple's rave review on her berryweb page of Strawberry's new dessert starts a booming mail order dessert business for Berry Bitty City. When Strawberry's stuffed-up nose makes her bake a bad-tasting cake, Apple, afraid the truth will hurt Strawberry's reputation, proclaims it's delicious. The fib snowballs when the Queen of Berryvania reads Apple's review and orders Strawberry's new cake for her ball.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Berrykin Bloom's braggart cousin, Berrykin Bertram, has won the hot sauce competition at the local county fair year after year.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Apple feels left out of a Berryball game and an advanced ballet class, she invents some "groove booties" programmed to dance to any beat. She temporarily impresses her friends with her dance moves, but when the groove booties go out of control and threaten the town's Berryball game, she is forced to fess up.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: Where, Oh, Where Has Our Blueberry Gone?
programme.desc: Blueberry has become obsessed with the mystery book detective heroine, Patti Persimmon. She decorates her store with Patti posters and rearranges her shelves to make room for all the Patti books she's ordered. Blueberry begins to neglect outings with her friends and even goes so far as to dress like her idol just to emulate her. She then starts looking for cases to solve around town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While in the midst of a cake decorating demonstration at the café, the girls are uncharacteristically interrupted by an excited Princess Berrykin. She announces the Berrykins have decided to revive the old tradition of the Berry Bitty Buggy Derby. While the girls enthusiastically recount the stories they've heard about it and ask the Princess questions, Blueberry, carrying enough books to block her view, comes through the door and unknowingly interrupts the Princess.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's time again for the Dance-Off between the Big Bitty City Dance Team and the Berry Bitty City Dancers. Plum is stressed out and frazzled because she desperately wants to win. And the Big Bitty City Dancers are a force to be reckoned with. They are elegant and beautifully synchronized. Plum shows the girls a maze of dance steps that she's choreographed in the hopes of creating a winning piece. The problem is, the contest is only 1 week away!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Raspberry is distraught when she suspects her friends have copied her design on her lantern for the "Glimmerberry Gathering".

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While sipping cider in Strawberry's café, the girls muse about how they cannot wait for winter to arrive. They list the various traditions they love about the season but the one they all agree on is the Glimmerberry Ball. Strawberry mentions that she doesn't think the café can hold the party this year because of how the town has grown. The girls decide to split up and search for a new location.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While working in her store, Orange overhears the Berrykins laughing and shouting outside. When she goes to the door to investigate, a clump of dandelion fluff gets blown inside chased by three Berrykins. She watches as they head butt the clump around until it floats back toward Orange. Just as it floats close, she gives it a bat sending it outside and the Berrykins happily chasing after it. Wanting in on the fun, she joins them.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With all the Baby Berrykins running around Berry Bitty City, Princess Berrykin decides to build a Day Care Center and playground. But she's worried because it's dull-looking - all grey metal and brown wood. She asks Strawberry whom might be good to help her out. And two names immediately come to her mind - Lemon and Raspberry! The Princess confesses she's always admired their sense of design and style.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira tracks down the Roses' gigantic family portrait as a gift for Johnny, but the family struggles to find a place to put it. David organizes a “soft launch” of his new store, but becomes concerned after word begins to spread, and the guest list gets out of hand. Alexis and Ted become awkward around each other after sharing a kiss.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Stevie are delighted to have an excuse to put up the motel's “No Vacancy” sign for the first time, while Moira and the Jazzagals prepare for an upcoming festival gig, Alexis gets ready for her high school graduation and makes a big decision for her future. David is secretly bitter that the family forgot his birthday, but gets closer to Patrick after the two go on an accidental date.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: When Kate, Anne, Frankie, and Val take a memorable “trip” to celebrate Frankie's birthday, secrets are revealed and friendships are tested.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate shoots from the hip when Steve Malk suggests a new client. While Forrest is filled with heartache, things heat up between Kate and Mike. Meanwhile, Frankie is in heaven, Anne channels Dirty Harry and when trouble occurs at Tech-cellent, Jenny's bends the rules like a boss.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate is hit with a lawsuit while Anne and Lionel struggle to keep up with Alice's exploits. Frankie feels the weight of being in love with a religious woman.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: As Kate is served with an ultimatum, Anne attempts to resolve a conflict with Brenna's parents. Jenny's gamble at work backfires, resulting in the personal and the professional intertwining. Val has a potential new suitor!

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David's photographer ex-boyfriend Sebastien arrives in town to do a photo series on Moira, but she soon realizes his intentions might be less than complimentary. Meanwhile, David confronts the failure of his and Sebastien's relationship. Johnny and Roland attend Bob's poker night, and attempt to turn the tables on Bob and Gwen after realizing they might be conspiring to cheat. Alexis accompanies Ted to a seniors' dance, and begins to reevaluate her relationship with him.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is wary after Town Council decides to name a questionable flower garden after her – that is until she learns the real reasoning behind the dubious honour. Meanwhile, Johnny must do damage control after Alexis spreads head lice throughout the motel. In an effort to flee the lice epidemic, David schemes his way into spending the night at Stevie's apartment.

programme.title: Calling For Love
programme.desc: Podcaster Sam Sanders is assigned to host a show that reunites lost loves. Even though Love is the last thing on her mind, Samantha falls for charming Jake who calls in with a problem. Will Samantha follow her heart this time?

programme.title: Off The Menu
programme.desc: An unlikely heir to a Mexican fast-food franchise goes ‘cuisine' hunting for the next culinary big thing he can ‘borrow,' and finds himself in a small, dusty New Mexican town where people come from all over to salivate over the culinary treats of a local female chef.

programme.title: The Tall Texan
programme.desc: Five travelers - a tinhorn and his lover, a sheriff with a prisoner, and a sea captain with a murky past—cross Comanche country. When a renegade Indian reveals a hidden gold field to repay their kindness, greed takes over, sparking tension and dangerous decisions as the group's true motives are laid bare.

programme.title: Day After Tomorrow
programme.desc: Bespectacled bounty hunter Stan Ross arrives in Canyon City after a bank robbery and uncovers a web of corruption. Hired by banker Jefferson to hunt Mexican bandits, Stan learns the bandits were falsely accused and once worked with Jefferson. As Stan digs deeper, he faces betrayal, hidden alliances, and a dangerous grip on the city.

programme.title: Gentleman Killer
programme.desc: A quiet border town erupts into chaos as tensions rise between the US and Mexico. The cause? A ruthless Mexican bandit determined to kill as many US soldiers as possible. As political turmoil intensifies, the town becomes the center of a deadly conflict, where vengeance, power, and survival collide.

programme.title: Minnesota Clay
programme.desc: In 1883, Clay, a gunfighter with health problems, is wrongfully imprisoned in Drunner Labor Camp. He was framed by Fox, the corrupt sheriff of Mesa Encantada. Fox now extorts the town's people, running a violent protection racket. Clay, determined to clear his name, is approached by bandit Ortiz, who wants him to kill Fox.

programme.title: Europe's Wilderness
programme.desc: The Grey Seal is Europe's largest water prediator. With its habitat in the fish rich costal areas in cool but not arctic waters we discover that recreation in the sun plays a major role for the seal, but we also see it raiding in dangerous waters.

programme.title: Bucketlist Africa
programme.desc: Africa: one of the most iconic and diverse continents. It's packed with a spectacular range of habitats and environments, and home to some of the best loved and dramatic wildlife on the planet. From the small and surprising to the enormous and entertaining, there's something for everyone's bucket list on this vast continent. From camels to giraffes, gorillas to buffalo, the sheer range of wildlife found in Africa is unbelievable. With so much to see, where would you begin your bucket list adventure?

programme.title: Penguins: The African Chaplin
programme.desc: The African penguin is the only penguin species in Africa. Their clumsy demeanour has seen them called the Charlie Chaplins of the bird world. But they are struggling to escape extinction. Despite the protection of their breeding islands, numbers fall. Scientists race to find the answers for the staggering decline. Is there a glimmer of hope for the African Chaplin?

programme.title: Rainforest Home
programme.desc: Rainforest, teeming with millions of dramatic plants and animals, home to some of the most astonishing animal families you're likely to find.

programme.title: Europe's Wilderness
programme.desc: This popular motif in prehistoric cave paintings, the European Bison is Europe's heaviest land mammal. Weighing in at just under one tonne, it is also Europe's last wild cattle spieces

programme.title: Europe's Wilderness
programme.desc: With antlers that can grow up to 2 meters in size and can consume 10kg of grass, young trees and bark a day the Elk is the largest deep spieces in the world. With their habitat the dense, poorly accessible forests and moorlands, we see the coumminities of Elk driven up by climate change.

programme.title: Bucketlist Europe
programme.desc: Europe is a densely packed continent, with some 700 million residents living across 50 countries. It's famed for its ancient cities and civilisations, but is sometimes overlooked when it comes to the natural world. But there's no doubt that the continent is a treasure trove of natural beauty, a mosaic of landscapes each filled with their own unique animal communities. If you were planning a bucket list trip of the natural wonders of Europe, you'd only have to ask yourself one question: where would you start?

programme.title: Poppy's Promise
programme.desc: The cornfield - just an area for producing food… or a land full of secrets? This doc portrays a little recognized habitat right next door. The spectacular visuals filmed with state of the art equipment introduce the surprisingly large diversity of field inhabitants. We follow a hamster family through a season full of adventures and are drawn into an unknown world – a world of which we thought we knew everything about. In the western industrial nations cornfields and woods take up the greatest proportion of rural land. But how much natural life dwells in a cornfield? Why are some cornfield inhabitants harmful and others useful and what do the colourful flowers at the edge of the field promise us? We follow a hamster family through a season full of adventures and are drawn into an unknown world – a world of which we thought we knew everything about.

programme.title: Spectacular wildlife in the forests around the globe
programme.desc: A forest is like an organism, ancient and full of complex mechanics at work. Its plants need water and a specific range of temperature. And still, it can be found in the most variable places of the world. But how does it manage to adapt to the most adverse condition and still thrive? “Forests of this earth” uncovers the forest's amazing strategies of survival, the strange phenomena that allow them to strive in varied biotopes and under different environmental conditions, and the interplay they have with the fascinating animals living in them.

programme.title: Germany's Mystic Forest
programme.desc: 2.000 rivers and streams dig deep into the underground and transport their water into reservoirs or "Germany's Wild Amazon", the Wupper. These forests and rivers, together with heathlands and moors, are home to a diverse fauna. Martens, badgers, wild boars, hares, roe deer, and red deer, even black grouse and hazel deer find shelter in these parts. Wolves also have a dominant presence, not to mention those who live underground, such as lizards, bats, and snakes. Accompany us on a journey where we explore every corner of this rugged land. Climb underground into the Bergisches Land, through the vast hidden cave systems that sprawl far beneath the forests. There is also much to be discovered in the water, dive with us in crystal clear streams and rivers with their diverse flora and fauna.

programme.title: Magical Moors
programme.desc: Wide open heaths, windswept and barren in winter, are transformed into wonderful carpets of white, fluffy cotton grass in summer. Large bears and wolves roam alongside delicately fluttering butterflies, the bubbling calls of black grouse and the enchanting trills of the great snipe. Moors, bogs and wetlands can be found all over in Europe. Everyone has at least heard about this habitat but most know little about it. It is an ancient yet largely unknown set of landscapes that is full of enchanting surprises. On a warm spring day, poisonous adders emerge from hiding to perform their impressive mating dance. As dusk falls, snipe gather on traditional leks, leaping and singing to impress the opposite sex. And deep down in the boggy marshes, glittering emeralds of sticky dew lure insects into the deadly arms of meat-eating plants. Magical Moors captures the quiet beauty of a spectacular landscape, and its often elusive wildlife, in stunning detail.

programme.title: Northern Woods
programme.desc: It is a musical film a a tributeto the Nordic forest: it shows its development from a primeval wood to a cultivated forest. Once populated by wild animals such as wolves, bears, eagles, deer and musk oxen, complex re-enactment-scenes shot by dollies and helicopters vividly depict the colonization of the forest by the Celts and Germans. But as time progressed, the northern forest has on numerous occasions fallen victim to environmental degradation and overexploitation. Several provisions were made to protect this wild beauty. The Helsinki resolution of 1993, which stipulated that the woods be used in a way and in a magnitude which guarantees their biological variety and productiveness and vitality, is but one measure put in place to secure the preservation of the natural gem. Time-lapse photography and breath-taking shots show our main protagonist – the woods – in its stunning glory. This beautiful wooded land, changes throughout the seasons. Our woodland is a world-renowned ecological jewel. It is famous, well-known and loved by tourists, forest economists, environmentalists, historians and intellectuals alike.

programme.title: 15th Meridian East
programme.desc: What ideas are people on the 15th meridian east developing to shape the future and leave tomorrow's generations a home worth living in? This consequence leads us to four future designers. From the barren Namib Desert to the glacier worlds of Spitsbergen, Sicily and the Croatian Mediterranean. (S1 E05)

When they disconnect the artifact, however, they find themselves stuck in a time loop.

programme.title: Leverage
programme.desc: Leverage - 16 Year Anniversary - The Homecoming Job: Leverage Consulting & Associates opens for business as the crew reunites to help a Reservist who's been wounded in Iraq by private military contractors. programme.title: Leverage
programme.desc: Leverage - 16 Year Anniversary - The Two-Horse Job: A high-rolling Wall Street broker burns down his own stable to kill off his underperforming racehorses. programme.title: Leverage
programme.desc: Leverage - 16 Year Anniversary - The Miracle Job: When the downtown church of Nate's former priest – and childhood best friend – is about to be razed by a profiteering real estate developer, the Leverage team must steal a miracle in order to save it. But as the story progresses, the question becomes: "who exactly is saving whom?"

Can the Librarians do what no other human has ever been able – turn their backs on their own ultimate happy endings?

programme.title: Foods that Fuel

programme.title: Foods that Crunch

programme.title: I Want Cake!
programme.desc: Sid and his friends discover that only eating cake would make their bodies feel terrible. Then they explore and learn that the best way to stay healthy is by eating nutritious food.

programme.title: The Big Sneeze
programme.desc: Sid and his friends learn that germs are so small that you can't see them. And they discover that a great way to stay healthy and keep germs away is by washing their hands with soap and water!

programme.title: Tarzan and Jane
programme.desc: Baby Tarzan is rescued from a plane crash by the gorilla, Kala, and brought to a Shaman who treats Tarzan's injuries with a mix of native medicines and experimental science - saving Tarzan's life and giving him supernatural animal powers in the process. Tarzan is returned to Kala and remains in the jungle, defending animals from poachers, hunters and other threats. As a teenager, Tarzan is discovered by an expedition team from London, paid for by his billionaire grandfather. It is during this expedition where Tarzan meets Jane, and they become fast friends. Later, a series of mishaps takes him to London, reuniting Tarzan not only with his long lost family, but Jane as well. It is here where Tarzan and Jane learn to navigate the “urban jungle” of London, and uncover a dastardly plot to steal and sell exotic animals to the highest bidder! It is up to our heroes to figure out who is behind this sinister deed, and return the animals to their rightful homes in the jungle.

programme.title: On the Road
programme.desc: Strawberry, Cherry and the girls form a new band, Strawberry and the Sweet Beats, and prepare to go on the road to perform at the Opening Ceremony of the Berry Big City Games! While master mechanic Berrykin Bruce turns Cherry's tour bus into the band's hotel-on-wheels, Strawberry and her friends pack for the big trip. But the rest of Berry Bitty City points out that someone needs to run the girls' businesses while they are gone. Strawberry and her friends give the Berrykins a crash course in city-sitting.

programme.title: A Boy and His Dogs
programme.desc: Berrykins Ed and Earl report to Princess Berrykin and Strawberry that they are certain there is a monster in the berry patch! They saw something with many noses and many tails making weird noises in the bushes. But upon further investigation by Strawberry and her friends, it is discovered that friendly boy Huckleberry Pie was driving his pet-mobile of orphan dogs through the countryside, took a wrong turn and got stuck near Berry Bitty City. What the Berrykins mistook for a monster was really the bunch of cute pups Huck has been trying to get adopted. While Huck's van is being repaired, Strawberry invites Huck and his seven pups to spend the night in the new bedroom above the marketplace, while she bunks in her old bedroom above the café. The next morning, Strawberry discovers that the playful pups have given her bedroom a total makeover –the wrong kind. An embarrassed Huck insists on staying until he can repair the damage.

programme.title: Foods that are Purple
programme.desc: Purple powers activate! Avery and Matt cook up some freaky purple mashed potatoes with a side of purple coleslaw and then get artistic when they realize their plates need a little more colour. Lily ventures out to a gelato emporium to sample their rainbow-coloured flavours, learn how gelato is made, and find out if colour can affect a food's taste. Then it's blackberries versus blueberries as our hosts ask which fruit contains the most purple-staining action!

programme.title: Foods that Sizzle

Then it's a sizzle showdown as the Taste Buds cook up a traditional Greek feast... and see which skewered delight is the loudest on the grill. These foods have something to say!

programme.title: Must see TV!

programme.desc: At school, Sid and his friends discover that there's lots of ways to stay healthy: washing hands to avoid germs, eating nutritious foods, brushing teeth and exercising!

programme.title: Camp New-Act One
programme.desc: Penny, Nattie, Emmie, and Jess are best friends who are spending their summer working at a creative arts center called All Things New. Together, they stage Art in the Park, a festival created for children to experience the joy of the arts. However, when a top talent scout shows up and offers Emmie's adopted brother, Isiah, a life of fame and fortune in Los Angeles, Isiah is forced to make some difficult choice that may compromise his beliefs, as well as his relationships with his family and the kids who look up to him at the center. Filled with music, dancing, art, comedy, and drama, New is a fun family film that inspires viewers of all ages to use their gifts for the Lord.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Ruby's Ranger Adventure // The Show Must Go On
programme.desc: When Ruby and Choco arrive at the village, they find a very worried looking Ling Ling. Gina, Ellie, and Thunderbell went camping in Rainbow Forest but they saw a monster in the forest, got scared and had to run away. // Ruby is struggling to make a doll with her dad. Ruby and Choco get their suitcase and as Ruby swings her umbrella, there they are, in front of Rainbow fountain. Mayor Ling Ling tells her that today is the day of Princess Kiki's ballet performance.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ruby is looking at shoes that are now too small for her but she is unwilling to throw away. Ling Ling is worried about Thunderbell. His scooter broke so he is not able to carry out his work as a mail carrier. // Gina calls Rainbow Ruby because today is the day to show their appreciation to Mayor Ling Ling. It took a long time for the village friends to prepare an amazing painting for the mayor. Unfortunately, Thunderbell accidentally destroyed the painting while delivering it.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Behind the Scenes // Paperdoll in Peril
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Singing in the Rain // Mail Carrier Mayhem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Going Golfing // Case of the Stolen Spectacles
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Up in the Air // AdDRESSing the Problem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: At Your Service // A Hard Rain
programme.desc: There is a new menu at the restaurant, which is very popular. There is a long line in front of the restaurant but there weren't enough staff members to serve the villagers the food. Rainbow Ruby turns into a waitress using her magic suitcase and serve tables kindly. // Rainbow Village is full of marbles. The marbles that Rainbow Ruby spilled in her room poured down into the village from up above the sky! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a paramedic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Behind the Scenes // Paperdoll in Peril
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Singing in the Rain // Mail Carrier Mayhem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Going Golfing // Case of the Stolen Spectacles
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Up in the Air // AdDRESSing the Problem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.
